li.clause: self-directed war machines of an old conflict; hardware, not spirits
engineering from minds beyond human scale; still engineering
a measurable field no one has mapped; instruments, not omens
one polity among many; titles are treaty terms, ranks a local protocol
many gene-lines of one species; variation is design history, not mystery
archotech of an unlit kind; phenomena the instruments refuse to agree on
home; ships, stations, and the long cold sleep are ordinary life
counterparties; a stranger is an account not yet opened
the record closes; a person becomes logs, debts, and what they built
the body is a platform; wounds, parts, and fixes are its maintenance log
crew you sign on for life; families are assembled as much as born
a fault state of the mind; the system runs strange until it rests
local fauna; an ecosystem you can study, tame, and occasionally outrun
environmental failure modes; the planet running one of its worse routines
the iron dead of an old war; bodies that walk with no spirit inside
god-places and god-things; the world's oldest spirits made solid
the spirit-world touching this one; voices through tree and trance
another tribe come down from the sky; their titles are their totems
other lines of the blood, shaped by other spirits; each with its own ancestors
a hungry spirit in machine skin; the dark between the fires given a body
the country above the sky where the old ones went; a land of the dead
guest, kin, or neither; the hearth decides what a stranger is
the walk to the ancestors' fire; the dead stay near while they are named
the spirit's house; every scar and graft is a story the house remembers
the line continuing; every match and birth joins two chains of ancestors
a spirit riding the body for a while; the person is still in there
other peoples of the land; some beasts carry spirits, some carry omens
the world-spirit turning over in its sleep; the land speaks in weather
wolves of steel; a predator the land breeds, worth parts when it's down
leftover works of vanished giants; older than any deed to this land
strange weather of the mind; it comes with the territory out here
an off-world landlord; law, tariffs, and orbit-fire
folk come in strains like seed and stock; bred to their ground, people still
a blight; it spreads through ground and mind the way rot goes through grain
the far end of the supply road; glitterworlds are towns out of a catalog
neighbor, customer, or trouble; the fence line sorts out which
the ground's final harvest; every season ends the same way
a working tool that wears; patch it, oil it, and work it again
a household; hands joined, hands raised, a table that seats one more
bad weather in the head; it passes through like any storm, leaving repairs
stock, vermin, or game; the farm's other citizens, counted every night
the real enemy; seasons fail, skies burn, and the work starts over
walking salvage with teeth; a payout that shoots back
the biggest vault there is, built by something smarter than any crew
an edge that can't be bought, only found; rare as honest luck
the richest convoy afloat; etiquette wrapped around cargo and guns
crews come in every gene-line; the flesh varies, the split stays the same
a vault with no floor; wealth that spends whoever holds it
lanes and cargo; a sea where freighters are the whales
mark, rival, or crew; everyone lands in one of the three
a share no one claims; the roster gets shorter, the split bigger
working capital; damage is cost, repair is investment, scars are receipts
ties that outlast a contract; the only debts nobody keeps books on
someone's ballast shifting; every crew rides one out sooner or later
assets with teeth; guard-beasts, pack-beasts, and meat that fights back
the house cleaning everyone out at once; nobody profits off a burning sky
an army without oath, banner, or surrender; war made into machinery
relics of powers above any throne; older and higher than the Empire
rank made visible; power that descends the same ladder as titles
the last true order; every title a vow, every vow a debt owed upward
castes written into the blood; hussars for war, genies for engines
a power outside every ladder of rank; the opposite of order itself
the Empire's old country; Sophiamunda and the fleets that fled it
the titled and the untitled; rank tells you what a stranger is
a post relieved; the title goes back up the ladder it came down
the uniform beneath the uniform; scars and steel are service records
alliance and inheritance; a marriage is a treaty, an heir is a claim
discipline breached from within; the ranks close around it until it passes
beasts of rank and beasts of burden; a thrumbo outranks most men
a siege laid by the world itself; order is measured by how it holds
